vitals.terrain: Coastal roads, valleys, mountains, rural communities
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple daily stops with breaks, meals

course_description.overview: 9-day, 8-night round-island cycling tour covering approximately 900 km, showcasing Taiwan's diverse landscapes including coasts, mountains, valleys, and cultural sites.

key_segments.name: North-Cross Island Highway
key_segments.description: Connects cities and rural communities, highlighting sustainable tourism.

key_segments.name: East Coast Pacific Views
key_segments.description: Features dramatic cliffs, green valleys, and small towns.

key_segments.name: Central Mountains
key_segments.description: Includes challenging climbs through tea hills and hot springs areas.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Multi-day endurance event with significant daily mileage, varied terrain from flat coasts to mountainous passes, suitable for social teams and experienced riders seeking cultural immersion.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Taipei; bike rentals, luggage support, and English-friendly services widely available.
logistics.accommodation: 8 nights included in tour (hotels/B&Bs), bike-friendly options.
logistics.registration_details: Part of Taiwan Cycling Festival; attracts teams and first-time island circumnavigators; check Taiwan Bike or Tourism Administration sites.
logistics.bike_logistics: E-bike friendly; rentals and support services available; consistent paved surfaces with clear wayfinding.
logistics.local_considerations: Low-carbon focus; night markets, hot springs, and cultural stops; typhoon season possible in fall; rider-friendly with plentiful snacks and coffee.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic 9-day circumnavigation blending epic Taiwanese scenery, culture, and social cycling vibes.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Endurance cyclists, teams, first-time island tourers, and those seeking a ride-cation with food and heritage.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for its diversity, accessibility, and status as Taiwan's flagship cycling event.
pro_tips: Train for 100km+ daily averages with climbs.
Pair with non-riding partner activities like night markets.
